char.name: Pareth
char.bio: Camel-driver on the long roads, Haran to Egypt and back, dust in my teeth the whole way. I've seen every city worth seeing. Ask me anything about the trade routes; I've got stories that would curl your beard.

friends.name: Boran
friends.bio: Trader at the city gate. Spices from the south, copper from the hills, fine linen, name it, I'll get it. Best prices in Canaan (don't tell my competition). The patriarchs' caravans have been very good for business.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Joseph
friends.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

friends.name: Pharaoh
friends.bio: Pharaoh, lord of the Two Lands, the living Horus. I dreamed of seven fat cows and seven lean. Who can tell me what it means? 🐄

friends.name: Simeon
friends.bio: Second son of Leah. Quick to draw the sword when my sister's honor is on the line.

posts.author: CHAR_673
posts.body: Word off the eastern road, and it is bad. Four kings out of the east came down and crushed the whole valley. Sodom and Gomorrah broke and ran. The kings stripped the cities of everything, food, goods, people, and turned back east with a train of captives a mile long. And here is the part that reaches us: Lot was living in Sodom. They took him too. Abram's own nephew, roped in with the rest of the plunder, marching east.

comments.author: CHAR_669
comments.body: I passed that column at a distance. Never seen so many people driven like cattle. Somebody has to tell Abram.

author_info.name: Boran
author_info.bio: Trader at the city gate. Spices from the south, copper from the hills, fine linen, name it, I'll get it. Best prices in Canaan (don't tell my competition). The patriarchs' caravans have been very good for business.

comments.author: CHAR_672
comments.body: Somebody already has. I have never in my life seen the old man move that fast.

author_info.name: Oziel
author_info.bio: Shepherd. If your flock drinks at MY well again, herdsman, there will be words, and maybe more than words. A man's water is a man's life out here. (Abram and Lot had the right idea: separate the camps before blood is spilled.)

author_info.name: Pareth
author_info.bio: Camel-driver on the long roads, Haran to Egypt and back, dust in my teeth the whole way. I've seen every city worth seeing. Ask me anything about the trade routes; I've got stories that would curl your beard.
